tests.test_name: Swift - PO - single test, core output structure
tests.description: Verifies that uploading a single Swift test file produces valid, well-formed documentation output with at least one named test case.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The tool produces visible output after processing a Swift file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All placeholders or template tokens in the output are fully resolved
The output correctly identifies the source language as Swift
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is assigned a meaningful name

tests.test_name: Swift - PO - struct+for data-driven expands one test per row
tests.description: Verifies that a data-driven Swift test using struct and for-loop patterns is expanded into one documented test entry per data row.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The tool produces visible output after processing a data-driven Swift file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All placeholders or template tokens in the output are fully resolved
The output correctly identifies the source language as Swift
Data-driven rows are each expanded into their own separate test entry, yielding at least 3 tests

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each expanded test case is assigned a meaningful name

tests.test_name: Swift - QAM - single test, coverage matrix populated
tests.description: Verifies that when a QA Manager analyzes a single Swift test file, the output includes a populated test coverage matrix for each test case.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The tool produces visible output after processing a Swift file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All placeholders or template tokens in the output are fully resolved
The output correctly identifies the source language as Swift
At least one test case is detected and documented from the uploaded file

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each documented test case is assigned a meaningful name

test_coverage.verification_step: First test has a non-empty array test_coverage_matrix
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The QA Manager role triggers generation of a coverage matrix with at least one entry

tests.description: Verifies that when a QA Manager analyzes a data-driven Swift test file, the multiple data rows are collapsed into a single test entry whose coverage matrix rows match the number of examples and follow the correct schema.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The tool produces visible output after processing a data-driven Swift file

test_coverage.verification_step: JSON output has no unresolved values
test_coverage.what_it_checks: All placeholders or template tokens in the output are fully resolved
The output correctly identifies the source language as Swift
Data-driven rows are collapsed into a single test entry under the QA Manager role

test_coverage.verification_step: Every test has a non-empty test_name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The collapsed test case is assigned a meaningful name

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix row count matches the examples
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The number of rows in the coverage matrix equals the number of data-driven input rows

test_coverage.verification_step: Coverage matrix rows use the correct schema for 'swift'
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Each coverage matrix row conforms to the expected Swift-specific structure
